head: f61d906 (v1.6.0)
host: rv (riscv64, 8 cores, 7.7 GiB RAM, Ubuntu 24.04 LTS Noble)

corpus.note: synthetic random vectors via DO loop with STORAGE MAIN; no real-embedding semantic structure

comparison_to_x86_64.ratio_estimate: ~10-25x slower on RISC-V vs x86 AVX2 (different corpus sizes; ratio is rough). The turbovec inner loop falls back to scalar on RISC-V; no RVV intrinsics available in turbovec 0.5.0 / fork.

verdict_reasoning: pg_turbovec v1.6.0 builds, installs, and queries cleanly on RISC-V 64 (Ubuntu 24.04). Tiny index correctness test passes (vector ORDER BY returns expected nearest neighbour). 50-query warm sweep returns 240-245 ms with stdev 0.73 ms - extremely tight distribution showing no thermal/scheduler interference. 5x compression ratio matches x86_64 numbers. Build/install path required LD_PRELOAD=libopenblas.so.0 because cblas_sgemm is a deferred symbol not in the .so's NEEDED entries; this is a packaging note for non-NixOS hosts.
ops_notes: rv lacks pgvector — comparison was pg_turbovec-only; x86 numbers from Phase U/V used as reference.
LD_PRELOAD required: postmaster started with LD_PRELOAD=/path/to/libopenblas.so.0
cargo-pgrx install + cargo pgrx init pg17 took ~7 hours total on rv; pg_turbovec compile only 18 minutes.
